skill.name: sop-api-development
skill.description: A two-week REST API development SOP that coordinates requirements, architecture, TDD implementation, testing, documentation, and deployment work.
skill.summary: Coordinate a complete REST API project from planning through production launch.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static command execution and weak cryptography alerts are false positives from JavaScript template literals and ordinary API planning words in SKILL.md. No malicious intent, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or executable shell logic was found. A medium warning remains because the SOP directs agents to handle database credentials, environment secrets, and production deployment steps.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Secret and Deployment Handling Requires Review
medium_findings.description: The SOP asks agents to store database credentials, configure environment secrets, and perform staging and production deployment work. This appears legitimate for API delivery, but users should verify secret storage, access controls, and deployment approval gates before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly instruct agents to manage credentials, secrets, and deployments. The context is a legitimate DevOps workflow, so this is a controlled operational risk rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are JavaScript fenced-code examples that use template literals inside Task calls. I found no shell execution, subprocess invocation, or user-controlled command construction in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are visible as JavaScript template delimiters in markdown code blocks. There is no executable Ruby, shell backtick operator, or command runner in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary words such as description, Design, and REST in prose and headings. The only real password-related guidance recommends bcrypt, and no weak algorithm implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas contain planning text, headings, or API documentation language, not cryptographic code. The surrounding context does not show MD5, DES, SHA1 use, or custom cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are API and Monitoring Checklist Text
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts map to normal API test cases, resource endpoint examples, staging validation, and OpenAPI compliance metrics. I found no commands that enumerate host users, processes, files, or network interfaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are endpoint examples and quality checks inside a REST API SOP. They do not instruct host reconnaissance or collection of system inventory.

content.user_title: Build REST APIs With a Two-Week SOP
content.value_statement: REST API projects often fail when planning, testing, documentation, and deployment happen out of order. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured SOP for coordinated API delivery.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a REST API project into four phases across a 14-day timeline.
Defines agent roles for product planning, architecture, database design, backend development, testing, DevOps, and documentation.
Uses a TDD workflow for endpoint tests, implementation, review, and refactoring.
Includes API security, performance, E2E testing, and production validation checkpoints.
Produces planned deliverables such as requirements, OpenAPI specs, database schema, test plans, runbooks, and deployment reports.
limitations: It is a process template, not a runnable API framework or generated codebase.
It assumes the user can provide requirements, infrastructure choices, and deployment permissions.
Secret handling and production deployment steps need human review before execution.
The timeline may be unrealistic for large, regulated, or legacy API projects.

use_cases.title: Plan a new backend API
use_cases.description: Create an ordered delivery plan with requirements, architecture, database design, and test strategy before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Coordinate multi-role API delivery
use_cases.description: Assign clear workstreams for backend, database, QA, DevOps, documentation, security, and production validation.

use_cases.title: Standardize API launch readiness
use_cases.description: Use testing, documentation, deployment, monitoring, and knowledge transfer checkpoints before a production release.

prompt_templates.title: Start API Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a small REST API project from requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the REST API development SOP for a user management API. Start with requirements, architecture, database design, and test planning.

prompt_templates.title: Create a TDD Endpoint Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Define implementation work for one endpoint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the SOP to the POST /api/auth/register endpoint. Produce the test-first workflow, implementation tasks, review checks, and expected deliverables.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Launch Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Review readiness before staging or production.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SOP to create a pre-production validation checklist for my REST API. Include tests, coverage, security, performance, documentation, monitoring, and rollback checks.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Full SOP
prompt_templates.scenario: Customize the workflow for a complex team or product.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this two-week REST API SOP for a multi-tenant billing API with PostgreSQL, webhooks, RBAC, audit logs, CI/CD, and strict release approval gates.

output_examples.input: Plan a user management REST API from scratch.
output: A phased delivery schedule from requirements through deployment.
Named roles for planning, architecture, database, backend, QA, DevOps, and documentation.
Expected deliverables such as requirements, OpenAPI spec, schema, tests, and runbooks.

output_examples.input: Create a launch checklist for an API release.
output: A pre-production validation checklist covering tests, coverage, security, performance, documentation, monitoring, and rollback readiness.
A staging validation plan with smoke tests, full test suite execution, data checks, and go or no-go criteria.

output_examples.input: Organize work for authentication and CRUD endpoints.
output: A TDD sequence for tests, implementation, review, and optimization.
Parallel workstreams for authentication middleware, CRUD resources, search, and webhook features.

best_practices: Provide concrete API requirements, data models, authentication rules, and deployment constraints before invoking the SOP.
Review every generated secret, credential, migration, and deployment action before applying it to a real environment.
Use the SOP as a coordination checklist and adapt timelines to project size, compliance needs, and team capacity.
anti_patterns: Do not let the skill deploy to production without a human approval gate and rollback plan.
Do not store real credentials in plain text or shared project notes.
Do not treat the two-week timeline as fixed for large or safety-critical APIs.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete REST API automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured SOP for planning, building, testing, documenting, and deploying an API.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning. The static alerts were false positives, but secret and deployment handling need review.

faq.question: What project size fits this SOP best?
faq.answer: It fits small to medium REST API projects with clear requirements and a team that can review generated work.

faq.question: Does it include API security testing?
faq.answer: Yes. The SOP includes OWASP API checks, injection testing, authorization testing, and rate limit validation.

faq.question: Can I customize the timeline?
faq.answer: Yes. The two-week schedule is a baseline and should be adjusted for complexity, compliance, and team capacity.
